title: Specify the Location of Image Files
breadcrumbs: Specify the Location of Image Files
text: Specify the Location of Image Files Images that are specified with the @ sign (for example, @donald.gif ) are loaded from the file system. By default, Uniface tries to load image files from the working directory, but you can specify a different default directory. To load the image from the working directory, just specify an image name. For example: @donald.gif To load the image from another location, specify the complete path for the image. For example: @C:/images/donald.gif Note: This is not recommended unless the file name and location are dynamically determined at runtime before assigning the value to the field. To specify a default directory for images (other than the working directory), edit the usys.ini file. Specify the directory using the Images setting in the [paths] section. You can also specify multiple directories. For example: [paths] images = D:/myimages, C:/images Copy Related Links Images Specifies the name of the directory where image files are stored. You can specify more than one directory by using a comma-separated list. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
